item.title: Being “Good” at Things 摘要
item.description:
The author reflects on a golf interview where pros define 'good' by score, but Collin Morikawa rejects numerical benchmarks, arguing that true skill lies in finishing every hole honestly without shortcuts like picking up short putts. The author extends this to programming, design, and writing: being 'good' means doing the hard, consistent, disciplined work that others avoid, not chasing arbitrary metrics. Quality is a disposition, not a number.
标签: golf, expertise, quality, discipline, craftsmanship, mindset, consistency, honesty, skill, reflection

item.title: DiffusionGemma 摘要
item.description:
Google has released DiffusionGemma, a new open-weight (Apache 2 licensed) Gemma model for faster text generation, available on Hugging Face as google/diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it. NVIDIA hosts the model for free on their NIM cloud API. The model achieves high speed, generating 2,409 tokens in 4.4 seconds (over 500 tokens/second), building on earlier experimental Gemini Diffusion research.
标签: Google, AI, generative-ai, LLMs, NVIDIA, Gemma, DiffusionGemma, open-source, text-generation, performance

item.title: Please, use a link! 摘要
item.description:
The author rants about single-page apps breaking browser navigation, specifically the back button. They argue that developers often misuse HTML elements like divs or buttons with onclick handlers instead of proper anchor tags with href attributes. This breaks browser history, accessibility, and user expectations. The post emphasizes using native <a> tags or framework-specific link components (e.g., React Router's Link) to preserve standard browser behavior like history, hover previews, and mobile options.
标签: web development, single-page apps, navigation, HTML, accessibility, React, user experience, browser history, best practices, rant

item.title: My Firefox addons as of Firefox 151 (and the current development version) 摘要
item.description:
The author reflects on their Firefox addon usage since 2022, noting minimal changes due to conservatism. They still use Foxy Gestures, uBlock Origin, uMatrix, Cookie AutoDelete, Stylus, Textern, Certainly Something, HTTP/2 Indicator, ClearURLs, and Open in Browser, but dropped HTTPS Everywhere. Due to switching to first-party isolation, they replaced Cookie Quick Manager with Cookie Manager. A second Firefox environment now uses Multi-Account Containers and FoxyTab for container-based browsing, plus additional addons like Copy Alt Text, Don't Fuck With Paste, Right-Click Borescope, and User-Agent Switcher. This second profile became persistent, prompting the addition of Cookie AutoDelete.
标签: Firefox, addons, browser extensions, privacy, containers, uBlock Origin, uMatrix, Cookie AutoDelete, Stylus, Multi-Account Containers

item.title: Formally proving a calculation with Claude and Lean 摘要
item.description:
John D. Cook describes an experiment where he used Claude to generate a Lean proof for a Fourier coefficient calculation involving Bessel functions, based on a blog post about Kepler's equation. Claude produced a proof after eight iterations of error correction, but the final proof contains four 'sorry' placeholders for standard analysis lemmas (integration by parts, integral of cosine, integrability, and change of variables) that exist in Mathlib. The post includes the full Lean code and explains the mathematical background.
标签: Lean, Claude, formal proof, Bessel function, Fourier series, Kepler equation, calculus, AI-assisted proof, Mathlib, integration
